_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that sentences are the way we put ideas together in speaking and writing.

_source.w: We are learning that sentences are the way we put ideas together in speaking and writing.
y: Sentences help us express complete thoughts that make sense.
Understanding sentences is essential for both reading and writing.
When we know what a sentence is, we can start creating our own.
eg: We learn that 'The boy sat on the dog' means something different from 'The dog sat on the boy' — word order matters!
We listen to a sentence and count how many words are in it.
We practise saying complete sentences instead of just single words.
We look at a sentence and identify where it starts and finishes.
We make up silly sentences by changing the words around and see how the meaning changes.
We clap once for each word in a sentence to feel its rhythm and length.
